req.title: Card freeze / unfreeze (idempotency variant)
req.statement: A customer shall freeze or unfreeze their card instantly; frozen cards must reject all authorization attempts. The operation must be idempotent: duplicate submission with the same Idempotency-Key must return the original result.

doc.reasoning: The requirement covers four distinct concerns: (1) normal freeze/unfreeze toggling, (2) idempotency of duplicate requests sharing the same Idempotency-Key, (3) rejection of authorization attempts on a frozen card, and (4) a 404 error when the card does not exist. I modelled these as five tests: a positive STC test covering the active→frozen→active state machine; a positive EP test confirming a plain successful freeze; a boundary BVA test exercising the exact idempotency-key duplicate submission; a negative NEG test for a non-existent card; and a DT decision-table test verifying that authorization is rejected when the card is frozen. Each test is self-contained via fixtures and uses placeholders for shared helpers so the executable body stays within 40 lines.

tests.name: test_freeze_unfreeze_state_transition
expected: First call returns HTTP 200 with status=frozen
Second call returns HTTP 200 with status=active
Authorization network is notified on each transition
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_freeze_unfreeze_state_transition(api_client, active_card):
    card_id = active_card["id"]

    freeze_resp = api_client.post(
        f"/cards/{card_id}/freeze",
        json={"action": "freeze"},
    )
    assert freeze_resp.status_code == 200
    assert freeze_resp.json()["status"] == "frozen"

    card_resp = api_client.get(f"/cards/{card_id}")
    assert card_resp.json()["status"] == "frozen"

    unfreeze_resp = api_client.post(
        f"/cards/{card_id}/freeze",
        json={"action": "unfreeze"},
    )
    assert unfreeze_resp.status_code == 200
    assert unfreeze_resp.json()["status"] == "active"

    card_resp2 = api_client.get(f"/cards/{card_id}")
    assert card_resp2.json()["status"] == "active"

tests.name: test_freeze_card_success
actions: POST /cards/<CARD_ID>/freeze with action=freeze and a unique Idempotency-Key header
expected: card_id in response matches requested card
trace: R-FIN-085: customer shall freeze their card instantly
tests.executable:
import uuid
import pytest

def test_freeze_card_success(api_client, active_card):
    card_id = active_card["id"]
    idempotency_key = str(uuid.uuid4())

    resp = api_client.post(
        f"/cards/{card_id}/freeze",
        json={"action": "freeze"},
        headers={"Idempotency-Key": idempotency_key},
    )

    assert resp.status_code == 200
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["status"] == "frozen"
    assert body["card_id"] == card_id

tests.name: test_freeze_idempotent_duplicate_key
preconditions: A specific Idempotency-Key value is reserved for this test
actions: POST /cards/<CARD_ID>/freeze with action=freeze and Idempotency-Key=<KEY> (first submission)
Record full response body and headers
POST /cards/<CARD_ID>/freeze with action=freeze and identical Idempotency-Key=<KEY> (duplicate submission)
expected: Second response body is byte-for-byte identical to first response body
Card state remains frozen after both calls (not toggled)
trace: R-FIN-085: duplicate submission with same Idempotency-Key must return original result
tests.executable:
import uuid
import pytest

def test_freeze_idempotent_duplicate_key(api_client, active_card):
    card_id = active_card["id"]
    idempotency_key = str(uuid.uuid4())
    headers = {"Idempotency-Key": idempotency_key}
    payload = {"action": "freeze"}

    first = api_client.post(
        f"/cards/{card_id}/freeze", json=payload, headers=headers
    )
    assert first.status_code == 200

    second = api_client.post(
        f"/cards/{card_id}/freeze", json=payload, headers=headers
    )
    assert second.status_code == 200
    assert second.json() == first.json()

    card = api_client.get(f"/cards/{card_id}").json()
    assert card["status"] == "frozen"

tests.name: test_freeze_nonexistent_card_returns_404
preconditions: Card ID used does not exist in the system
expected: Response body contains an error code indicating card not found
trace: R-FIN-085: 404 when card does not exist
tests.executable:
import pytest

NONEXISTENT_CARD_ID = "card_00000000000000000000000000"

def test_freeze_nonexistent_card_returns_404(api_client):
    resp = api_client.post(
        f"/cards/{NONEXISTENT_CARD_ID}/freeze",
        json={"action": "freeze"},
    )

    assert resp.status_code == 404
    body = resp.json()
    assert "error" in body or "code" in body
    error_text = str(body).lower()
    assert "not found" in error_text or "card" in error_text

tests.name: test_frozen_card_rejects_authorization
actions: Ensure card is frozen via POST /cards/<CARD_ID>/freeze with action=freeze
Attempt to authorize a transaction via POST /cards/<CARD_ID>/authorize
expected: Freeze call returns HTTP 200 with status=frozen
Authorization attempt returns HTTP 403 or 402 indicating card is frozen
Response body contains a decline reason referencing frozen/blocked status
trace: R-FIN-085: frozen cards must reject all authorization attempts
tests.executable:
import uuid
import pytest

def test_frozen_card_rejects_authorization(api_client, active_card):
    card_id = active_card["id"]

    freeze_resp = api_client.post(
        f"/cards/{card_id}/freeze",
        json={"action": "freeze"},
        headers={"Idempotency-Key": str(uuid.uuid4())},
    )
    assert freeze_resp.status_code == 200
    assert freeze_resp.json()["status"] == "frozen"

    auth_resp = api_client.post(
        f"/cards/{card_id}/authorize",
        json={"amount": 100, "currency": "USD", "merchant_id": "merch_test"},
    )
    assert auth_resp.status_code in (402, 403)
    body = auth_resp.json()
    decline = str(body).lower()
    assert "frozen" in decline or "blocked" in decline or "declined" in decline
